title: Why Did Sea Limited (SE) Stock Drop -23% Today?
author: tickeron_community
selftext:
# Key Takeaways

* [SE](https://tickeron.com/ticker/SE/) shares plunged approximately **23%** at Tuesday's open, marking one of the steepest single-session selloffs in recent company history
* The primary catalyst was a **severe Q4 2025 earnings miss**: adjusted EPS of **$0.63** fell well short of the analyst consensus of **$0.80**, a miss of roughly 21%
* Revenue beat expectations at **$6.9 billion** versus a $6.49 billion estimate, up 38.4% year-over-year — but the EPS shortfall dominated investor reaction
* Accelerating competition from **TikTok Shop and Lazada** is squeezing Shopee's margins, forcing elevated spending on promotions, cashbacks, and logistics
* Management's 2026 guidance — Shopee GMV growth of \~25% and full-year adjusted EBITDA "at least flat" with 2025 — fell short of market expectations for more aggressive profitability expansion
* Traders are watching for analyst price target revisions and any early read on Q1 2026 e-commerce trends across Southeast Asia

# Opening Summary

Sea Limited ([SE](https://tickeron.com/ticker/SE/)), the Singapore-headquartered technology conglomerate behind the Shopee e-commerce platform, the Garena gaming division, and the Monee digital financial services unit, cratered approximately 23% at the open on Tuesday, March 3, 2026. The stock dropped from its prior close of $108.45 to trade near $83.51 in early going. The immediate trigger was the company's Q4 and full-year 2025 earnings report, released before the bell, which delivered an EPS figure widely regarded as disappointing despite record headline revenue growth.

# Earnings Miss: The Primary Catalyst

Sea reported Q4 2025 adjusted earnings per share of **$0.63**, missing the Wall Street consensus of **$0.80** by approximately 21%. This was not an isolated stumble — Sea had now missed EPS estimates in multiple consecutive quarters, with prior-quarter misses averaging in excess of 40% against analyst expectations. Repeat earnings disappointments of this scale tend to trigger outsized selling as investors lose confidence in near-term profitability visibility.

Net income for the quarter came in at $410.9 million, up 72.9% year-over-year, while adjusted EBITDA rose 33.2% to $787.1 million. Although these figures represented genuine growth, the gap between reported EPS and consensus was wide enough to spark aggressive unwinding of positions that had been built on expectations of accelerating profitability.

# Competition Eroding Margins

Bloomberg reported that the earnings shortfall directly reflected the cost of battling intensifying rivals in Southeast Asia's e-commerce market. Shopee, Sea's largest business unit, faces relentless pressure from **ByteDance's TikTok Shop** and **Alibaba's Lazada**, both of which have deployed deep-pocketed promotional strategies to win market share. To defend its position, Shopee has ramped up marketing expenditures, cashback programs, and logistics investments — all of which compress operating margins quarter after quarter.

Operating expenses surged in recent quarters as Sea leaned heavily into customer acquisition and retention spending. The company's fintech arm, Monee, while growing revenues 54.3% year-over-year to $1.1 billion, has also seen provisions for credit losses balloon significantly as its loan book expands, adding further drag to the bottom line.

# Guidance Disappoints Growth-Oriented Investors

Management's forward outlook failed to restore confidence. For fiscal 2026, Sea guided Shopee's GMV to grow **approximately 25% year-over-year**, and projected full-year adjusted EBITDA to be **no lower than 2025 levels in absolute dollar terms**. The phrase "no lower than" signals caution rather than expansion, and for a stock trading at a premium growth multiple, investors interpreted this language as a de facto acknowledgment that margin improvement could stall.

Analysts at Wedbush had already trimmed their price target on [SE](https://tickeron.com/ticker/SE/) from $190 to $170 ahead of the print, maintaining an "Outperform" rating. Further analyst revisions are expected to follow the session's selloff.

# Market Context and Trading Activity

Volume in [SE](https://tickeron.com/ticker/SE/) was dramatically elevated relative to its average at the open, consistent with an earnings-driven capitulation event. The stock's beta of approximately 1.54 amplifies moves during high-conviction selling episodes. The decline was entirely idiosyncratic — driven by company-specific earnings news rather than any parallel weakness in the broader market or the Southeast Asian technology sector.

From a technical standpoint, the gap-down open pushed [SE](https://tickeron.com/ticker/SE/) through multiple layers of support, including the $105 level that had held through the prior session. The stock had already shed more than 45% from its 52-week high of $196.50 reached in September 2025 before today's move, and today's action extended that correction sharply.

# Trending AI Robots

For traders navigating high-volatility events like today's sharp move in [SE](https://tickeron.com/ticker/SE/), Tickeron's platform offers hundreds of AI-powered trading bots covering thousands of tickers across sectors and market caps. Not all bots perform equally in every market regime, which is why Tickeron curates a dedicated [Trending AI Robots](https://tickeron.com/bot-trading/trending-robots/) page highlighting only the strongest-performing bots under current conditions. These bots differ by strategy, time horizon, traded symbols, and performance metrics — giving traders a broad menu of options whether they seek momentum, mean-reversion, or earnings-driven strategies. Traders looking to bring systematic, data-driven discipline to fast-moving markets are encouraged to explore the Trending AI Robots section and identify a bot suited to their goals.

# What Comes Next for SE

The immediate focus will be on analyst reactions and price target adjustments following the earnings miss and guidance disappointment. With management now guiding for "at least flat" EBITDA in 2026, the debate will center on whether Sea can re-accelerate profitability once its competitive spending cycle peaks, or whether structural margin compression has set in. Shopee's ability to sustain 25% GMV growth without further eroding per-unit economics will be a closely watched metric across Q1 and Q2 2026.

Longer-term risks include continued currency headwinds across Sea's operating markets, the pace of loan default normalization at Monee as its credit book matures, and the sustainability of Garena's bookings recovery. Investors will also monitor whether Wedbush and other covering analysts maintain their Outperform ratings or follow the stock lower with further cuts. [SE](https://tickeron.com/ticker/SE/)'s next scheduled earnings date — covering Q1 2026 — will be the first opportunity for management to demonstrate whether the 2026 guidance trajectory is tracking or deteriorating.
